description: Regional branch of the Islamic State active in Central and South Asia, primarily Afghanistan and Pakistan. Founded on 26 January 2015 by former Tehreek-i-Taliban Pakistan members. Seeks to establish a caliphate in the historic Khorasan region. Responsible for numerous terror attacks, including the 2021 Kabul airport attack and the 2024 Crocus City Hall attack in Russia. Currently led by Shahab al-Muhajir.

country_links.evidence_quote: Azerbaijan's State Security Service announced that it had arrested three militants affiliated with ISKP who were allegedly planning an attack on the Israeli embassy in Baku
country_links.context: ISIS–K members were arrested in Azerbaijan for planning an attack on the Israeli embassy.

country_links.evidence_quote: Individuals from Myanmar and Bangladesh have also been part of it, and one known Bangladeshi national of ISIS–K was arrested by the Afghan intelligence agency in 2020.
country_links.context: Individuals from Bangladesh have been part of ISIS-K, and one Bangladeshi national was arrested by Afghan intelligence.

country_links.evidence_quote: Other foreign fighters have included Indians, with 14 Keralites having been freed by the Taliban from prison following the fall of Kabul.
country_links.context: Indians have been part of ISIS-K, with 14 Keralites freed from prison by the Taliban.

country_links.evidence_quote: a twin bombing on 3 January 2024 against a gathering of Iranians mourning the death of former Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps (IRGC) Quds Force (QF) commander, Qasem Soleimani, at his grave in Kerman, Iran
country_links.context: ISIS–K carried out a twin bombing in Kerman, Iran.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Taliban also claimed that two Malaysians of ISIS–K were caught by them following a gun battle in Kabul on 26 August 2021.
country_links.context: Two Malaysians of ISIS-K were caught by the Taliban after a gun battle in Kabul.

country_links.evidence_quote: Individuals from Myanmar and Bangladesh have also been part of it
country_links.context: Individuals from Myanmar have been part of ISIS-K.

country_links.evidence_quote: twin suicide bombings in July 2018 that killed at least 131 at election rallies in Pakistan, twin bombings in July 2016 that killed 97 Shia Hazara protestors in downtown Kabul, and a suicide bombing in July 2023 that killed 63 in Khar , Pakistan at a JUI (F) rally.
country_links.context: ISIS-K carries out terror attacks in Pakistan, including twin suicide bombings in July 2018 and a suicide bombing in July 2023.

country_links.evidence_quote: ISIS–K actively seeks external funding from sympathetic patrons, including wealthy individuals from Gulf countries such as Qatar and Saudi Arabia
country_links.context: ISIS–K receives funding from wealthy individuals in Qatar.

country_links.evidence_quote: In March 2024, four Tajik ISIS–K gunmen launched an attack on a concert hall in Krasnogorsk , Russia, with rifles and incendiaries, killing 145 and marking the group's first attack beyond Afghanistan's neighbors.
country_links.context: ISIS-K gunmen attacked a concert hall in Krasnogorsk, Russia, killing 145.

country_links.evidence_quote: ISIS–K actively seeks external funding from sympathetic patrons, including wealthy individuals from Gulf countries such as Qatar and Saudi Arabia
country_links.context: ISIS–K receives funding from wealthy individuals in Saudi Arabia.

country_links.evidence_quote: ISIS–K conducted its second external operation against Tajikistan... the group launched seven rockets from a Katyusha launcher from the Afghan-Tajik border in Takhar Province targeting the 'headquarters of the apostate Tajik army.'
country_links.context: ISIS–K launched rockets from Afghanistan into Tajikistan.

country_links.evidence_quote: In June 2024, US officials arrested eight Tajik men in Los Angeles , New York , and Philadelphia , who were reportedly involved in an ISIS–K plot within the United States, with connections to a larger ISIS–K cell being monitored in Central Europe . All eight were said to have entered the United States illegally across the U.S. border with Mexico .
country_links.context: ISIS-K had a plot within the United States, with connections to a larger cell in Central Europe, and members entered the US illegally across the Mexico border.

country_links.evidence_quote: the group launched ten rockets out of a Katyusha launcher from northern Afghanistan, over the Amu Darya (Oxus) river into the Uzbek border city of Termez
country_links.context: ISIS–K launched rockets from Afghanistan into Uzbekistan.
